name: Abhishek Bhattacharjee
bio: Professor of Computer Science at Yale University. Introduced BCIs to the systems community and leads the Yale Systems Architecture Lab. ACM SIGARCH Maurice Wilkes Award recipient. Expert in computer architecture and systems at all scales, from data centers to implantable brain-computer interfaces.

name: Raghavendra Pothukuchi
bio: Assistant Professor at UNC Chapel Hill. Blavatnik Regional Award recipient and ISCA Best Paper awardee. Expert in computer architecture, brain-computer interfaces, and cognitive modeling. Co-developed the HALO and SCALO BCI processors with the Yale team.

name: Tim Buschman
bio: Professor at Princeton Neuroscience Institute. National Academy of Sciences Troland Research Award recipient. Leader in large-scale electrophysiology, neural motifs, and CNMF methods. Leads rodent brain interfacing experiments to validate Acorn.

name: Rajit Manohar
bio: Professor at Yale University. Misha Mahowald Prize for Neuromorphic Engineering recipient. Recognized leader in asynchronous VLSI design with decades of chip tape-out experience. Co-designed the HALO BCI processor.

name: Lin Zhong
bio: Professor at Yale University. ACM SIGMOBILE RockStar Award recipient. Director of the Yale Efficient Computing Lab. Expert in operating systems, runtimes, and disentangled system software for fault-tolerant embedded systems.

name: Lori Bruce
bio: Associate Director at Yale’s Interdisciplinary Center for Bioethics. Former consultant to the Presidential Commission for the Study of Bioethical Issues. Leads ethics research thrust, Community Bioethics Forum, and bioethics training across the expedition.

name: Anurag Khandelwal
bio: Assistant Professor at Yale University. NSF CAREER awardee. Expert in in-network memory management, address translation, and datacenter systems. Contributes to Thrusts 2 and 3 on hardware-software co-design for Acorn.

name: Arun Natarajan
bio: Professor at Yale University. NSF CAREER and DARPA Young Faculty awardee. Expert in RF circuits, correlators, and margin computation for approximate analog computing. Co-leads Thrust 4 on wireless communication and power delivery.

name: Qiang Liu
bio: Assistant Professor at Yale University. Apple Research Fellow and Simons Institute participant. Expert in dynamic, distributed, and parallel graph algorithms. Contributes to MoMA design and hardware accelerator partitioning in Thrusts 1 and 2.

name: Jay Lim
bio: Assistant Professor at UC Riverside. ACM SIGPLAN John C. Reynolds Doctoral Dissertation Award recipient. Expert in numerics, program synthesis, and correctly rounded math libraries. Co-leads education bootcamps and contributes to Thrusts 1–3.

name: Henry Duwe
bio: Assistant Professor at Iowa State University. NSF CAREER awardee. Head of the Embedded Architecture and Systems Lab and ChipForge co-curricular. Leads VLBI simulator development, REU program, and education outreach representing the EPSCoR community.

name: Ronald Coifman
bio: Professor at Yale University. National Medal of Science recipient and pioneer in data transforms for signal processing and hashing. Contributes to Thrust 1 on neural signal processing methods and MoMA sub-kernel identification.
